walkthrough.title: Sort the Two Card Types
walkthrough.text: Alright, let's get Hasty Baker set up. Open the box and you'll find two types of cards. The blue-backed cards are the big main deck — about 110 cards total with fun cartoon ingredient characters and strategy cards mixed in. The red-backed cards are your recipe cards — there are 24 of these, and each one shows a dessert with four ingredients listed on it. Go ahead and separate the blue cards from the red cards into two piles.
walkthrough.image_caption: The blue-backed main deck cards with the Hasty Baker logo

walkthrough.title: Shuffle and Deal the Blue Cards
walkthrough.text: Take the big stack of blue cards and give it a good shuffle. Now deal 7 cards face-down to each player. These are the cards in your hand — go ahead and look at them, but keep them secret from everyone else. Place the remaining blue cards face-down in the center of the table. That's your draw pile.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Discard Pile
walkthrough.text: Leave a spot right next to the draw pile for a discard pile. It starts empty, but cards will go here face-up throughout the game whenever someone plays a strategy card or replaces a card from their hand.

walkthrough.title: Deal the Recipes
walkthrough.text: Now grab the red recipe cards and shuffle those separately. Deal one recipe card face-up to each player. Place it on the table in front of you so everyone can see what dessert you're baking. The rest of the recipe cards go face-down in their own pile off to the side — that's the recipe draw pile.
walkthrough.image_caption: A recipe card for Cake — lists the four ingredients needed: Milk, Sugar, Frosting, and Flour

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Baking Station
walkthrough.text: Leave some space above your recipe card. That's where you'll place your ingredient cards as you collect them — think of it as your baking station. Off to the side of your recipe, leave room for your completed recipe pile. That's where finished recipes and any bonus point cards will go.
walkthrough.image_caption: An ingredient card — Flour with its cartoon character. Cards like these go in your baking station

walkthrough.title: Check the Table and Start
walkthrough.text: Take a quick look around the table. Each player should have 7 blue cards in their hand, one recipe card face-up in front of them, and an empty baking station above the recipe. The blue draw pile and an empty discard pile sit in the center, and the red recipe draw pile is off to the side. The player to the left of the dealer goes first, and play moves clockwise. You're all set — let's start baking!

summary.title: Sort the Two Card Types
bullets: Separate blue-backed cards (main deck, about 110 cards) from red-backed recipe cards (24 cards)
Blue cards have ingredients and strategy cards mixed together

summary.title: Shuffle and Deal the Blue Cards
bullets: Shuffle all blue cards and deal 7 to each player
Place remaining blue cards face-down as the draw pile

summary.title: Set Up the Discard Pile
bullets: Leave a spot next to the draw pile for a face-up discard pile
Starts empty — cards go here when played or replaced

summary.title: Deal the Recipes
bullets: Shuffle red recipe cards separately
Deal 1 recipe face-up to each player
Remaining recipes go face-down as the recipe draw pile

summary.title: Set Up Your Baking Station
bullets: Space above your recipe = baking station for played ingredients
Space to the side = completed recipe pile for finished recipes and bonus cards

summary.title: Check the Table and Start
bullets: Each player: 7 cards in hand, 1 face-up recipe, empty baking station
Center: blue draw pile + empty discard pile
Side: red recipe draw pile
Player left of dealer goes first, play clockwise

walkthrough.title: Your Turn — Two Actions
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you get to perform exactly two actions. Each action can be one of three things: play an ingredient card, play a strategy card, or replace a card from your hand. You can even do the same type of action twice if you want. Let's walk through each option.

walkthrough.title: Playing an Ingredient
walkthrough.text: Look at your recipe card — it lists four ingredients you need to collect. If you have one of those ingredients in your hand, you can place it face-up above your recipe card as one of your actions. You only need one of each ingredient unless someone plays a Double Batch card on you — but we'll get to that in a moment. The Wild Ingredient card is your best friend here. It can stand in for any ingredient on your recipe, and you never have to say which one it's replacing.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Wild Ingredient card — can substitute for any ingredient on your recipe

walkthrough.title: Playing a Strategy Card
walkthrough.text: Strategy cards are the spicy part of this game. They let you mess with other players or give yourself a boost. There are eight different strategy cards in the deck, and each one does something different. Playing a strategy card uses one of your two actions, and most strategy cards go straight to the discard pile after you play them. The two exceptions are Double Batch and Blue Ribbon — those stay on the table next to a recipe.

walkthrough.title: The Strategy Cards — Steal, Spoil, and Take
walkthrough.text: Here are the attack cards. Steal Ingredient lets you grab any ingredient card another player has in play and immediately put it in play for your recipe. The catch — it has to be an ingredient your recipe actually needs, and it goes straight to your baking station, not your hand. Wild Ingredients can be stolen, but Blue Ribbon and Double Batch cannot. Spoiled Ingredient is a big move — call out any ingredient name and everyone who has that ingredient in play must discard it, including you. Cards in hands are safe, and Wild Ingredients cannot be spoiled. Take 2 From Hand lets you randomly grab two cards from another player's hand and add them to yours.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Steal Ingredient card — move any one ingredient in play to your recipe

walkthrough.title: The Strategy Cards — Hasty Baker, New Hand, and Broken Timer
walkthrough.text: Hasty Baker is the card that shares the game's name. When you play it as one of your actions, you get two additional actions that turn. You can even play multiple Hasty Baker cards in the same turn for even more bonus actions. New Hand makes a player discard their entire hand and draw 7 fresh cards — you can play it on yourself or another player. And then there's Broken Timer, the nuclear option. Pick any player and they have to discard their current recipe and all the ingredients they've played, then start over on a new recipe. You can even play it on yourself if you're stuck.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Hasty Baker card — gives you 2 extra actions on your turn

walkthrough.title: The Strategy Cards — Double Batch and Blue Ribbon
walkthrough.text: These two cards stay on the table instead of going to the discard pile. Double Batch gets placed next to any player's unfinished recipe — now that player needs two of each ingredient instead of one. Only one Double Batch per recipe, and when the recipe is finished, the Double Batch card counts as an extra point. Blue Ribbon gets placed next to your own unfinished recipe. When you finish that recipe, the Blue Ribbon counts as an extra point in your completed pile. Only one per recipe.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Double Batch card — doubles ingredients required and stays on the table as a bonus point

walkthrough.title: Replacing a Card
walkthrough.text: If your hand is full of cards you don't need right now, you can spend one action to discard a card from your hand face-up onto the discard pile and draw a new one from the draw pile. Here's the important thing — playing the new card you just drew is a separate action. So if you replace a card with your first action and draw something perfect, you can play it as your second action.

walkthrough.title: End of Your Turn
walkthrough.text: After you've taken your two actions, check your hand size. If you have fewer than 7 cards, draw from the draw pile until you're back to 7. If you have more than 7, discard down to 7. Quick heads up — if you ever run out of cards in your hand completely during the game, immediately draw 7 new cards. Don't wait until the end of your turn. And if the draw pile runs out, shuffle the discard pile to make a new draw pile.

walkthrough.title: Completing a Recipe
walkthrough.text: Once you've played all four ingredients your recipe needs — or all eight if someone hit you with a Double Batch — your recipe is complete! Discard the ingredient cards from your baking station, then move the recipe card to your completed recipe pile. If you had a Blue Ribbon or Double Batch attached, those go into the completed pile too as extra points. Immediately draw a new recipe from the recipe pile and place it face-up in front of you. Here's a key timing rule — if you complete a recipe on your first action, draw your new recipe before taking your second action.
walkthrough.image_caption: A completed Chocolate Cake recipe — requiring Chocolate, Frosting, Butter, and Flour

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: Each completed recipe is worth 1 point. Each Blue Ribbon card in your completed pile is worth 1 point. Each Double Batch card in your completed pile is also worth 1 point. The first player to reach 5 points wins the game! That's all there is to it — collect ingredients, complete recipes, dodge the strategy cards, and bake your way to victory.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Blue Ribbon card — worth +1 bonus point when your recipe is completed

Blue Ribbon cards = 1 point each
Double Batch cards = 1 point each
First to 5 points wins

walkthrough.title: Protect Your Wild Ingredients
walkthrough.text: Here's the biggest first-game trap: playing your Wild Ingredient card early. It feels great to slap it down and get one step closer to finishing your recipe, but the moment it's on the table, everyone with a Steal Ingredient card is eyeing it. Wild Ingredients are the most valuable cards in the game because they work for any recipe. The smart play is to hold your Wild Ingredient until you can finish the recipe in the same turn. If you have three ingredients already played and a Wild in your hand, you can complete the whole recipe before anyone has a chance to steal it.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Wild Ingredient — the most valuable and most targeted card in the game

walkthrough.title: Time Your Bonus Cards
walkthrough.text: Blue Ribbon is a free extra point, but it paints a target on your back. If you play it with only one ingredient down, opponents have plenty of turns to hit you with a Broken Timer and wipe everything out — including that Blue Ribbon. The power move is to hold Blue Ribbon until you're about to finish. Play it as your first action, then play your final ingredient as your second action. Recipe complete, Blue Ribbon secured, no chance for sabotage. Same idea with Double Batch on yourself — only play it when you're confident you can finish the doubled recipe quickly.

walkthrough.title: Use Strategy Cards at the Right Moment
walkthrough.text: Broken Timer is the most powerful card in the game, and there's only one in the entire deck. Don't waste it when someone only has one ingredient down — save it for when an opponent has three or four ingredients on the table and is about to finish. That's maximum disruption. Double Batch is the same way — it's devastating when someone is at 4 points and about to win, because it forces them to collect double the ingredients and buys the whole table extra time. But if you play it too early, you might actually help them earn the bonus point.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Broken Timer — the most powerful card in the deck. Only one exists

walkthrough.title: Use the Hasty Baker Card Wisely
walkthrough.text: The Hasty Baker card is incredibly powerful — it gives you two extra actions when you play it. That means you can take four actions in a single turn. If you're holding a Hasty Baker and you have the ingredients to finish your recipe, you can play Hasty Baker as your first action, then play three ingredients in a row. Or play Blue Ribbon plus two ingredients. The key is to chain your Hasty Baker turn into a big finishing play that opponents can't react to.
walkthrough.image_caption: The Hasty Baker card — chain it with ingredients for a devastating finishing turn

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Opponents
walkthrough.text: This game rewards paying attention. Look at what ingredients your opponents need and think about what's likely in their hands. If you see someone with three ingredients down, it might be time to play that Steal Ingredient or Spoiled Ingredient before they finish. Also keep an eye on completed recipe counts. When someone hits 3 or 4 points, the whole table should shift into defense mode. That's when strategy cards become more valuable than playing your own ingredients.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Here are the things that trip up most new players. First, playing Wild Ingredients too early — you're basically handing it to whoever has a Steal card. Second, hoarding strategy cards when you should be playing ingredients. Strategy cards are great, but the game is won by completing recipes, not by holding a hand full of attacks. Third, ignoring what other players are doing. If everyone at the table focuses only on their own recipe, the first person to draw good cards wins. The interaction is what makes this game fun — use your strategy cards!

summary.title: Protect Your Wild Ingredients
bullets: Wild Ingredient is the most valuable card — works for any recipe
Don't play it early or it will get stolen
Hold until you can finish the recipe in the same turn

summary.title: Time Your Bonus Cards
bullets: Blue Ribbon paints a target — hold until you can finish the recipe immediately
Play Blue Ribbon as action 1, final ingredient as action 2
Same principle with Double Batch on yourself — finish quickly or it backfires

summary.title: Use Strategy Cards at the Right Moment
bullets: Broken Timer: save for when opponent has 3-4 ingredients down (max disruption)
Double Batch: best as defense when someone is about to reach 5 points
Only 1 Broken Timer in the whole deck — don't waste it

summary.title: Use the Hasty Baker Card Wisely
bullets: Hasty Baker gives 2 extra actions (4 total that turn)
Chain into big finishing plays: Hasty Baker + ingredients + Blue Ribbon
Multiple Hasty Bakers stack for even more bonus actions

summary.title: Watch Your Opponents

When someone hits 3-4 points, shift to defense mode

bullets: Playing Wild Ingredients too early = easy theft target

entries.definition: One of the eight ingredient types. There are 12 Flour cards in the deck, making it the most common ingredient.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Frosting cards in the deck.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Butter cards in the deck.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Eggs cards in the deck.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Sugar cards in the deck.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Chocolate cards in the deck.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Milk cards in the deck.
One of the eight ingredient types. There are 8 Berries cards in the deck.

A special ingredient card that can substitute for any ingredient on your recipe. It never needs to be designated as a specific ingredient. Wild Ingredients can be stolen but cannot be spoiled. There are 8 in the deck.

Strategy card. Take any ingredient card in play from another player and immediately put it in play for your recipe. The ingredient must be one your recipe needs. Goes directly to your baking station, not your hand. Wild Ingredients can be stolen, but Blue Ribbon and Double Batch cannot. There are 7 in the deck.

Strategy card. Call out any ingredient name. Everyone who has that ingredient in play — including you — must discard it. Cards in players' hands are not affected. Wild Ingredient cards cannot be spoiled. There are 4 in the deck.

Strategy card. Randomly take two cards from another player's hand and add them to yours. Playing any card you took costs a separate action. The other player waits until end of their turn to replenish. There are 4 in the deck.

Strategy card. Target player discards their entire hand and draws 7 new cards. Can be played on yourself or another player. Playing a card from the new hand costs a separate action. There are 4 in the deck.

Strategy card. When played as one of your actions, you gain 2 additional actions that turn. Multiple Hasty Baker cards can be played in the same turn for even more extra actions. There are 8 in the deck.

Strategy card. Choose any player — they must discard their current recipe and all ingredients in play, then draw a new recipe and start over. The discarded recipe goes to the bottom of the recipe pile. Can be played on yourself. There is only 1 in the entire deck.

Strategy card. Place next to any player's unfinished recipe. That player now needs two of each ingredient instead of one. Only 1 per recipe. When the recipe is completed, the Double Batch card goes into the completed pile as an extra point. There are 2 in the deck.

Strategy card. Place next to your own unfinished recipe. When the recipe is completed, the Blue Ribbon card goes into your completed pile as an extra point. Only 1 per recipe. There are 4 in the deck.

Red-backed cards showing a dessert and listing the four ingredients needed to complete it. Each recipe requires exactly four different ingredients (doubled if a Double Batch is attached). There are 24 recipe cards featuring 12 different desserts, with 2 copies of each.
The area above your recipe card where you place ingredient cards as you collect them. Only ingredients matching your current recipe can be placed here.
The pile off to the side where you place finished recipes along with any attached Blue Ribbon or Double Batch cards. Each card in this pile is worth 1 point.

Ingredient cards placed above your recipe in your baking station are considered in play. Cards in your hand are not in play. Strategy cards that affect ingredients in play only target the baking station, not hands.
Worth 1 point. A recipe is complete when all 4 required ingredients are played (or 8 with Double Batch). Discard ingredients, move recipe and any attached bonus cards to completed pile.
Each Blue Ribbon card in your completed recipe pile is worth 1 additional point. Only counts when attached recipe is completed.
Each Double Batch card in your completed recipe pile is worth 1 additional point. The player who completes a doubled recipe earns the bonus.
First player to reach 5 points wins. Points come from completed recipes (1 each), Blue Ribbon cards (1 each), and Double Batch cards (1 each) in your completed recipe pile.
